description: A dark, high-contrast ICE infographic comparing the number of officers needed to arrest a 'criminal alien' in cooperative versus uncooperative jurisdictions, promoting compliance with ICE detainers. The background is a black textured/asphalt surface with a faint photograph of metal handcuffs lying in the lower-right area. The top headline in white reads 'Number of law enforcement officers needed to arrest a criminal alien:'. Two labeled banner tabs split the layout: a red banner 'UNCOOPERATIVE JURISDICTIONS' above a large white '8 to 10', and a blue banner 'COOPERATIVE JURISDICTIONS' above a large white '2', separated by a vertical divider. Supporting body text reads: left - 'ICE needs more officers to help protect the public from unpredictable behavior by an at-large criminal attempting to evade justice.'; right - 'ICE can arrest a criminal alien with as few as two officers in a safe, controlled location, like a jail or court lockup area.' The bottom-left shows the DHS seal with 'U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement' and bottom-right the URL 'ICE.gov/detainers.' Propaganda technique: a stark statistical contrast (red=bad/danger vs blue=safe/efficient) arguing that sanctuary/non-cooperative policies endanger officers and the public, pressuring local jails to honor detainers. Target audience: the public, local law enforcement, and policymakers in sanctuary jurisdictions.
notable_text: Number of law enforcement officers needed to arrest a criminal alien: UNCOOPERATIVE JURISDICTIONS 8 to 10 / COOPERATIVE JURISDICTIONS 2 / ICE.gov/detainers
